title: PrivacyMode
description: Canonical privacy-mode primitive for terminal-first analytics contracts. Reference `#/definitions/PrivacyMode` instead of re-declaring the enum per schema, to stop cross-schema drift. `safe_agent_projection` (the default) restricts a payload to client-safe, IP-allowlisted projections — coarse labels, counts, and safe example references only, never raw proprietary signal values or raw prompt/response text. `elevated` is an owner-gated mode that MAY surface additional, still-bounded detail; the backend decides whether to honor it and a payload requested as `elevated` is never assumed to be elevated unless the response echoes it.

PrivacyMode.description: Privacy projection level for an analytics payload. Defaults to `safe_agent_projection` (client-safe). `elevated` is owner-gated and backend-honored, never client-asserted.
